title: Best VPN for Business and Remote Teams in 2026: What Enterprise VPNs Offer That Consumer VPNs Don't
description: Consumer VPNs and business VPNs solve different problems. Here's the honest breakdown of what you actually need for a remote team in 2026.
content:
## Why Businesses Need Different VPN Solutions Than Consumers

A consumer VPN solves one problem: hiding your traffic from your internet provider and unblocking geo-restricted content. You download an app, tap connect, and you're done.

A business VPN needs to solve five different problems at once, and most consumer VPNs fail at four of them.

**1. Centralized management**

An IT admin who needs to deploy a VPN to 50 laptops cannot ask every employee to download an app and configure it themselves. They need a management console where they can provision accounts, enforce settings, push updates, and revoke access when someone leaves the company. Consumer VPNs add a basic admin dashboard when they build a "Teams" version, but it is still a long way from real enterprise management.

**2. Business-grade logging and audit trails**

Healthcare companies, financial institutions, and legal firms often have legal obligations to log who accessed what and when. A HIPAA audit or a financial compliance review will ask for connection logs. Consumer VPNs advertise no-logs policies as a selling point. Business VPNs need the opposite: detailed, tamper-proof audit logs you can produce on demand.

**3. Split tunneling at the policy level**

Split tunneling sends corporate traffic through the VPN while personal traffic goes direct. Consumer VPNs offer this, but you configure it yourself on your own device. A business VPN lets the IT admin configure split tunneling per application, company-wide, without relying on individual employees to get it right. If an employee ignores the setting or disables it, corporate traffic is exposed. Policy-level enforcement removes that risk.

**4. Static IP addresses**

Many business tools, from banking portals to SaaS enterprise dashboards to cloud servers, whitelist specific IP addresses as a security measure. If your team connects from different rotating VPN IPs every day, those tools reject the connection. A business VPN with a dedicated static IP means your whole team appears from the same address every time, and that address can be whitelisted once.

---

## Consumer VPNs That Work for Small Businesses (1-10 People)

If you have a small team and your main needs are secure remote access and basic centralized billing, consumer VPN Teams editions are a reasonable starting point.

**NordVPN Teams** runs about $7 per user per month. You get a central admin console, dedicated static IPs, and the same server network as the consumer product. For a small team that needs secure connections and consistent IP addresses, this works.

**Surfshark for Teams** comes in around $6 per user per month and adds team management with device controls. The feature set is similar to NordVPN Teams.

**ExpressVPN for Teams** scales easily and has excellent client apps across every platform, which matters when your team mixes Windows, Mac, and mobile devices.

These products add central billing and basic admin panels. They do not give you real enterprise management, granular audit logs, or the kind of access control larger organizations need.

---

## True Business VPN Solutions

Once you pass about 20 users or enter a regulated industry, you need a real business VPN solution.

**Cisco AnyConnect** is the enterprise standard. It runs on existing Cisco network infrastructure, which means if your company already runs Cisco routers and firewalls, AnyConnect is effectively free. The setup is complex and requires a network engineer, but the result is a highly configurable, audit-ready VPN that integrates with your entire network stack. Most large enterprises use this or have used it.

**Palo Alto GlobalProtect** integrates with the PAN-OS firewall platform. If you're already running Palo Alto at the network perimeter, GlobalProtect extends that security to remote devices. It enforces host checking (verifying a device meets security standards before connecting), which Cisco AnyConnect also does but GlobalProtect makes easier to configure. This is the choice for security-focused enterprises.

**Tailscale** is the modern answer for remote-first companies that don't want to manage traditional VPN infrastructure. It creates a mesh network where each device gets a permanent private IP address. Devices connect directly to each other rather than routing through a central server, which makes it faster and simpler than traditional hub-and-spoke VPN architecture. Setup takes minutes rather than days. Pricing starts at $6 per user per month. Tech startups and remote-first companies choose Tailscale because it works without needing a network engineer to set it up.

**Cloudflare Zero Trust / WARP for Teams** takes a different approach entirely. Rather than a traditional VPN tunnel, it uses Cloudflare's global network to proxy connections to specific applications. The first 50 users are free, which makes it one of the most accessible enterprise-grade options available. It runs faster than traditional VPN tunnels because traffic routes through Cloudflare's optimized edge network rather than back-hauling through a central company server.

---

## The Shift From VPN to Zero Trust

Traditional VPN works on a simple principle: once you authenticate and connect, you're inside the network. You can access servers, internal tools, and file shares as if you were sitting at a desk in the office. The problem with this model is that one compromised credential gives an attacker access to everything inside the perimeter.

Zero Trust flips that assumption. Instead of trusting everyone inside the network, you authenticate per application, every time. Access to the HR system requires separate authentication from access to the code repository. Even if an attacker gets into one system, they cannot move laterally to others.

Microsoft (with Entra ID), Google (with BeyondCorp), and Cloudflare (with Zero Trust) are all pushing this model as the successor to traditional corporate VPN. Gartner predicts that by 2027 more than 50 percent of new enterprise deployments will use zero-trust architecture rather than traditional VPN.

For small businesses, this is not something to think about yet. For companies above 100 employees, especially in regulated industries, the question is no longer whether to adopt Zero Trust but when.

---

## What to Actually Use for a 10-Person Startup

Here's the honest recommendation for a small remote-first company that wants real security without a network engineer on staff.

**Tailscale at $60 per month for 10 users** gives you a mesh VPN where every team member's device gets a permanent private IP. Developers can access the production database by private IP. You can spin up a cloud server and add it to the mesh in two minutes. The admin console is clear and doesn't require networking knowledge. This is the best balance of simplicity and capability for tech companies.

**Cloudflare Zero Trust at no cost for your first 50 users** is the right choice if you want enterprise-grade access control without a budget for it. You define which users can access which applications, enforce device health checks, and get detailed access logs. The setup takes a few hours rather than a few minutes, but the result is more secure than a traditional VPN and scales better.

For either option, the setup is documented well, doesn't require Cisco certifications, and works on every device your team uses. If you're a 10-person startup choosing between NordVPN Teams and either of these two options, pick one of these two.

---

## Quick Comparison

| Solution | Best For | Price | Complexity |
|---|---|---|---|
| NordVPN Teams | 1-10 people, basic needs | $7/user/mo | Low |
| Surfshark Teams | 1-10 people, budget option | $6/user/mo | Low |
| Tailscale | Tech teams, remote-first | $6/user/mo | Low-medium |
| Cloudflare Zero Trust | Any size, free under 50 users | Free / $7/user/mo | Medium |
| Cisco AnyConnect | Enterprise, existing Cisco infra | Free with Cisco | High |
| Palo Alto GlobalProtect | Enterprise, security-focused | With PAN-OS license | High |

The right answer depends on your size, industry, and existing infrastructure. For most teams under 50 people, Tailscale or Cloudflare Zero Trust covers everything you need without the setup overhead of enterprise solutions.
